automotive-ev-battery.title: Honda to withdraw from Canada projects
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Honda has decided to suspend its multi-billion-dollar EV investment project in Canada indefinitely, following a postponement already announced for 2025. The company is currently in discussions with the Canadian government regarding the suspension of these significant electric vehicle manufacturing plans.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Chinese EV sales recover as exports surge
automotive-ev-battery.summary: China sold 1.34 million electric vehicles and plug-in hybrids in April, demonstrating renewed growth both year-over-year and across 2025. Strong export figures are driving the market expansion, though Chinese manufacturers continue to face headwinds in the domestic market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Norwegian cell manufacturer Morrow Batteries files for insolvency
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Morrow Batteries, a Norwegian battery manufacturer that recently opened an industrial facility for LFP cell production in 2024, has filed for insolvency and initiated bankruptcy proceedings for itself and its subsidiaries. The company is attempting to preserve at least a portion of its operations through the restructuring process.

automotive-ev-battery.title: It's official: Tesla Semi features 822 kWh battery capacity
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Official documentation from the California Air Resources Board has confirmed the battery specifications for Tesla's newly launched Semi trucks in series production. The Standard Range version features a 548 kWh battery, while the Long-Range variant is equipped with an 822 kWh capacity.

automotive-ev-battery.title: E.ON moves to acquire OVO
automotive-ev-battery.summary: E.ON has announced plans to acquire UK energy supplier OVO to strengthen its retail operations and accelerate flexible energy services centered on EV charging, home batteries, and smart tariffs. The acquisition is intended to support development of a more digital and customer-focused energy system across the United Kingdom.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Car rental company Car Inc Orders up to 100,000 BYD vehicles
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has signed a framework agreement with Chinese car rental company Car Inc for delivery of up to 100,000 vehicles. The partnership also includes an agreement to install BYD fast-charging infrastructure at selected car rental locations throughout China.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 370 Tesla Semi Trucks Ordered
automotive-ev-battery.summary: WattEV has placed an order for 370 Tesla Semi trucks, with over 300 units designated for deployment through a joint program with the Port of Oakland. The order represents significant fleet electrification activity in heavy-duty commercial transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Source taps Siemens and Evolt for EV charger maintenance
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Source has partnered with Siemens eMobility and Evolt Charging to establish a unified maintenance model for its UK and Ireland EV charging network. The collaboration aims to optimize network uptime and service reliability across both markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Lucid partner Nuro receives approvals for robotaxi pilot operations
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Autonomous driving specialist Nuro has received regulatory approval to commence passenger testing of robotaxis on California public roads. The pilot project represents progress toward the launch of a joint robotaxi program involving Lucid, Nuro, and Uber.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GWM starts production of the Ora 5 in Thailand
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Great Wall Motor has commenced domestic production of the Ora 5 compact SUV in Thailand, marking the beginning of overseas manufacturing for the Ora brand. This expansion represents GWM's strategy to establish regional production capacity outside China.

science-space.title: New James Webb Space Telescope images reveal how massive star clusters can reshape galaxies
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has captured unprecedented images revealing how massive star clusters influence galactic evolution and reshape their host galaxies. These observations provide critical insights into fundamental processes governing galaxy formation and development across cosmic time.

science-space.title: Hubble Survey Sets Up Roman's Future Look Near Milky Way's Center
science-space.summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope is conducting a comprehensive survey of the Milky Way's galactic bulge to establish baseline observations for the upcoming Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The dense region surrounding the galactic center contains billions of stars and objects that will be studied in unprecedented detail with Roman's advanced capabilities.

science-space.title: NASA's Artemis 3 rocket is taking shape for 2027 launch to test lunar landers
science-space.summary: The core stage for NASA's Artemis 3 rocket has been installed in the Vehicle Assembly Building and awaits engine integration ahead of its planned 2027 launch. This mission will carry the next generation of lunar landers and astronauts as part of NASA's sustained lunar exploration campaign.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche Mission Captures Mars During Gravity Assist Approach
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured detailed images of Mars during its May 15, 2026 gravity assist maneuver while positioned approximately 3 million miles from the planet. The assistance will adjust Psyche's trajectory and accelerate it toward its primary target, the metallic asteroid Psyche.

science-space.title: How NASA lunar scientists taught Artemis 2 astronauts to see the moon with different eyes
science-space.summary: NASA lunar scientists trained Artemis 2 astronauts to document and describe their observations of the Moon using specialized language that enhanced scientific understanding of the lunar environment. The astronauts' detailed observations during the historic crewed lunar flyby revealed surprising findings about the Moon's characteristics.

science-space.title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
science-space.summary: NASA and industry partners are developing next-generation radiation-hardened processors to enhance onboard spacecraft computing capabilities beyond the legacy systems used since the Apollo era. These advances in space computing are critical for coordinating and executing complex functions required for mission success in deep space exploration.

science-space.title: Sun unleashes colossal solar flare and coronal mass ejection, raising the chances of northern lights this week
science-space.summary: An M5.7 solar flare and accompanying coronal mass ejection triggered radio blackouts over the Atlantic and enhanced conditions for auroral displays in higher latitudes. This geomagnetic event demonstrates the Sun's influence on Earth's magnetosphere and space weather patterns.

science-space.title: NASA's SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
science-space.summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch to deliver approximately 6,500 pounds of scientific investigations and supplies to the International Space Station via Dragon spacecraft. The resupply mission will dock autonomously to the station's forward port to support ongoing research operations.

science-space.title: Nicholas Houghton: Engineering Crew Safety for NASA's Artemis Missions
science-space.summary: NASA engineer Nicholas Houghton designs critical safety systems for the Orion spacecraft's crew survival capabilities supporting Artemis missions, including the successful Artemis II lunar flyby. His work as part of the Orion Crew Survival Systems team exemplifies NASA's commitment to protecting astronauts during deep space exploration.

science-space.title: China's Tianzhou 10 freighter delivers 7 tons of cargo to Tiangong space station
science-space.summary: China's Tianzhou 10 cargo spacecraft successfully delivered nearly seven tons of supplies to the Tiangong space station on May 10, 2026, launching atop a Long March 7 rocket. The resupply mission arrived approximately five hours after launch, demonstrating the reliability of China's space logistics capabilities.

world-news.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
world-news.summary: US President Trump has criticized Iran's counteroffer to end the war, describing the month-long ceasefire proposal as "unbelievably weak." The statement signals potential breakdown in diplomatic negotiations between the two nations at a critical juncture.

world-news.title: How the Trump-Xi summit could set superpower relations for many years to come
world-news.summary: President Trump's visit to China represents the most significant US-China diplomatic engagement in years, with potential implications for long-term bilateral relations and global geopolitical stability. The summit comes amid ongoing tensions over trade, technology, and regional security issues.

world-news.title: US in closely-guarded talks to open new bases in Greenland
world-news.summary: The United States is engaged in confidential negotiations to establish three military bases in southern Greenland, according to multiple officials familiar with the discussions. The move reflects broader US strategic interests in Arctic security and countering potential adversarial influence in the region.

world-news.title: EU, UK sanction Russians over deportations of Ukrainian children
world-news.summary: The European Union and United Kingdom have imposed sanctions on Russian institutions and officials accused of systematic deportation and indoctrination of Ukrainian children during the ongoing war. The measure targets those responsible for what Western powers characterize as violations of international humanitarian law.

world-news.title: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
world-news.summary: The European Union has approved sanctions targeting violent Israeli settlers and settler organizations following months of diplomatic deadlock on the issue. The move comes as the EU's foreign policy leadership emphasizes that "extremism and violence carry consequences," though Israel has characterized the sanctions as "arbitrary."

world-news.title: China should stop hoarding food and fertiliser, says former World Bank chief
world-news.summary: Former World Bank President David Malpass has called on China to cease accumulating food and fertilizer supplies, citing concerns about global food security and market stability. The criticism emerges as US President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping prepare for high-level bilateral talks in Beijing.

world-news.title: 'Britain at the heart of Europe': How Starmer's plans are going down in the EU
world-news.summary: British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has outlined plans to rebuild and strengthen the UK's relationship with Europe, pledging a renewed commitment to European engagement in a defiant speech. The initiative signals a shift in UK-EU relations and broader European geopolitical positioning.

world-news.title: Could the latest violence in DR Congo undermine truce efforts?
world-news.summary: Lesser-known armed groups have conducted a series of attacks in the Democratic Republic of Congo's northeast region, raising concerns that escalating violence could derail ongoing ceasefire and peace negotiation efforts. The incidents underscore the fragility of regional security arrangements in central Africa.

world-news.title: Haiti's PM casts doubt on presidential vote by August as gang clashes grow
world-news.summary: Haiti's Prime Minister Alix Didier Fils-Aime has expressed skepticism about holding presidential elections by August due to escalating gang violence and widespread instability. The delay in electoral processes reflects the deteriorating security situation and humanitarian crisis affecting the nation.

world-news.title: Rebranding Francafrique
world-news.summary: France is undertaking a rebranding initiative regarding its historical relationship with Africa during a summit with African heads of state in Kenya. The effort reflects attempts to reshape France's diplomatic positioning and influence across the African continent amid evolving geopolitical dynamics.

tech-ai.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
tech-ai.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group identified and stopped a zero-day exploit created with AI that was intended for mass exploitation of an unnamed open-source web administration tool. The vulnerability would have allowed attackers to bypass two-factor authentication, marking the first time Google has publicly documented an AI-developed zero-day exploit.

tech-ai.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
tech-ai.summary: General Motors is restructuring its IT workforce, laying off hundreds of employees while hiring specialists in AI-native development, data engineering, cloud infrastructure, and model development. The move reflects the automotive industry's accelerating pivot toward artificial intelligence capabilities.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak, a security-focused AI initiative designed to detect and patch code vulnerabilities before attackers can exploit them. The tool uses the Codex Security AI agent to create threat models, identify attack paths, and automate detection of high-risk vulnerabilities, directly competing with Anthropic's recently announced Claude Mythos.

tech-ai.title: Here's what Mira Murati's AI company is up to
tech-ai.summary: Thinking Machines, founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, announced its development of interaction models that enable real-time collaboration with AI through simultaneous processing of audio, video, and text inputs. The technology aims to replicate natural human-to-human collaboration patterns rather than sequential single-thread interactions.

tech-ai.title: Finally, texts between Android and iPhone users can be end-to-end encrypted
tech-ai.summary: Apple has enabled end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging for iPhone users communicating with Android devices as part of iOS 26.5 beta release. The milestone represents a significant win for Google's long-standing push for RCS adoption and enhances privacy protections across the two dominant mobile platforms.

tech-ai.title: There aren't enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
tech-ai.summary: Cowboy Space secured $275 million in funding to address a critical infrastructure bottleneck in the AI computing boom by developing space-based data centers. The startup aims to overcome the shortage of launch capacity and high costs associated with deploying data centers in orbit around Earth.

tech-ai.title: Daniel Ek-backed defense tech Helsing to raise $1.2B at $18B valuation
tech-ai.summary: European military drone startup Helsing is nearing completion of a substantial funding round that would value the company at $18 billion. The five-year-old company, backed by Spotify founder Daniel Ek, is capitalizing on increased defense technology investment.

tech-ai.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
tech-ai.summary: The revived Digg platform is launching as an AI-powered news aggregator designed to identify and surface influential voices and genuinely important stories from the information deluge. The service aims to help users navigate content overload by algorithmically filtering for quality sources and narratives worth attention.

tech-ai.title: A million baby monitors and security cameras were easily viewable by hackers
tech-ai.summary: Security researchers discovered that approximately one million Meari-branded baby monitors and security cameras were vulnerable to unauthorized access, allowing bad actors to view private footage of homes and families. The widespread vulnerability exposed intimate moments without adequate authentication or encryption protections.

tech-ai.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
tech-ai.summary: Robinhood has filed confidentially for a second venture fund targeting both early-stage startups and growth-stage companies, capitalizing on the ongoing artificial intelligence investment boom. The move expands the retail brokerage's investment operations beyond its core trading platform.

finance-markets.title: Fuel, munitions and food: Trump's Iran war rips across US economy
finance-markets.summary: The ongoing conflict with Iran is imposing substantial economic costs on the United States, with businesses and consumers facing hundreds of billions of dollars in lost output. The disruption affects critical sectors including energy, defense, and agriculture, creating widespread macroeconomic headwinds.

finance-markets.title: US considers suspending fuel tax as Iran war pushes up prices
finance-markets.summary: The Trump administration is exploring emergency measures to combat fuel price inflation stemming from the Iran conflict, with Republican Senator Josh Hawley introducing legislation to suspend the federal fuel tax. The proposal reflects growing concern over the economic impact of elevated energy costs on consumers.

finance-markets.title: US Releases Another Wave of Emergency Oil as Gas Prices Bite
finance-markets.summary: The United States Strategic Petroleum Reserve awarded 53.3 million barrels to major oil traders and refiners as part of broader efforts to stabilize fuel prices during the Iran conflict. The emergency release underscores the administration's reliance on reserve depletion to manage inflationary pressures from the Strait of Hormuz closure.

finance-markets.title: Oil Extends Gain as Trump Casts Doubt Over Ceasefire With Iran
finance-markets.summary: Crude oil prices extended gains as President Trump rejected Tehran's latest peace offer, casting doubt over the ceasefire and prolonging the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The persistent uncertainty continues to support elevated energy prices globally.

finance-markets.title: Central Banks Tap Most Yuan Swap Lines With PBOC in Two Years
finance-markets.summary: Global central banks' utilization of People's Bank of China currency swap lines reached a two-year high in the first quarter, signaling increased international demand for the yuan and growing reliance on Chinese monetary mechanisms. This trend reflects shifting dynamics in global reserve currency composition and international financial relationships.

finance-markets.title: Oil Gains on Iran Deadlock, Treasuries Hold Losses: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Crude oil gained and Treasury bonds declined as geopolitical tensions with Iran persisted, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed and energy prices remaining elevated. The market dynamics reflect broader concerns about stagflationary pressures emerging from the conflict.

finance-markets.title: Gold Steady as Traders Track Hormuz Stalemate, Inflation Risks
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices remained stable as traders assessed geopolitical risks and inflation implications stemming from the Strait of Hormuz deadlock and elevated oil prices. The precious metal's steady performance reflects balanced risk perceptions regarding safe-haven demand versus inflationary concerns.

finance-markets.title: A weakened Trump arrives at Xi's court
finance-markets.summary: President Trump's diplomatic position in China is weakened amid ongoing international tensions, while Beijing holds substantial leverage in potential negotiations. China may pursue a strategy of symbolic but substantively limited agreements while maintaining its long-term strategic objectives.

finance-markets.title: Petrobras Misses Profit Estimates Despite War-Driven Oil Rally
finance-markets.summary: Brazil's state-controlled Petrobras fell short of earnings expectations despite benefiting from elevated oil prices, as the company maintained domestically controlled gasoline prices during the global conflict-driven price surge. The earnings miss highlights the fiscal constraints imposed by price control policies on national oil producers.

finance-markets.title: Japan's Reserves Tally Likely Too Early to Reflect Intervention
finance-markets.summary: Japan's foreign currency reserves remained relatively unchanged from the previous month, suggesting that recent currency intervention transactions occurred too late in the period to be captured in April-end data. This technical timing issue obscures the full extent of Japanese monetary authorities' recent market activities.

crypto-web3.title: Banking groups escalate fight over stablecoin yield ahead of Senate vote
crypto-web3.summary: The American Bankers Association has intensified warnings that the Senate's Clarity Act could trigger deposit flight into stablecoins unless lawmakers implement tighter yield restrictions. The regulatory dispute highlights mounting tension between traditional banking and crypto sectors as critical legislation advances toward a Senate vote.

crypto-web3.title: Upcoming crypto bill vote shifts fight to full Senate, 'major obstacles' remain TD Cowen says
crypto-web3.summary: The Senate Banking Committee is poised to vote on the Clarity Act crypto legislation on Thursday despite significant pushback from banking interests and Democratic lawmakers. Analysts note major obstacles remain before the bill can advance to the full Senate floor.

crypto-web3.title: American Bankers Association CEO makes final-hour push for tightened limits on stablecoin rewards ahead of Senate committee vote
crypto-web3.summary: The ABA CEO sent a letter to bank executives days before a critical Senate committee vote on crypto legislation, urging stricter limitations on stablecoin rewards to prevent deposit migration. The move underscores the banking industry's sustained opposition to unregulated stablecoin competition.

crypto-web3.title: Kraken parent Payward seeks fresh funding at $20 billion valuation ahead of planned IPO
crypto-web3.summary: Payward, the parent company of crypto exchange Kraken, is pursuing a new funding round while maintaining a $20 billion valuation as it expands through acquisitions in derivatives and stablecoins. The funding effort signals the company's preparation for an eventual initial public offering.

crypto-web3.title: Ripple raises $200 million from Neuberger Berman to expand its Ripple Prime platform
crypto-web3.summary: Ripple announced a $200 million investment from global investment firm Neuberger Berman to scale its Ripple Prime platform, which has tripled revenue since its $1.25 billion acquisition. The funding demonstrates institutional confidence in Ripple's enterprise blockchain and payment solutions.

crypto-web3.title: 'I don't think that's crazy': Here is why Circle is betting on new $3 billion blockchain
crypto-web3.summary: Circle, issuer of the USDC stablecoin, is backing a new $3 billion blockchain called Arc designed to serve as Wall Street's infrastructure for payments and tokenized finance. While analysts view the venture as speculative, Circle's investment signals growing institutional appetite for blockchain-based financial rails.

crypto-web3.title: Corpay taps BVNK to bring stablecoin wallets to corporate payments
crypto-web3.summary: Corpay has partnered with BVNK to integrate stablecoin rails into its treasury operations, aiming to improve capital efficiency and reduce reliance on pre-funded accounts. The integration reflects growing enterprise adoption of stablecoins for cross-border and domestic fund transfers.

crypto-web3.title: The biggest consensus overhaul in Solana history is officially live for testing
crypto-web3.summary: Solana's Alpenglow upgrade, representing the most significant consensus mechanism changes in the network's history, is now live on a community test cluster. The development indicates continued technical evolution of the Solana blockchain ahead of mainnet deployment.

crypto-web3.title: Galaxy-backed Boundary to launch 'verifiable' institutional stablecoin USBD
crypto-web3.summary: Galaxy Ventures-backed Boundary has secured $2 million in pre-seed funding to develop USBD, a verifiable institutional stablecoin scheduled to launch on Ethereum in early summer 2026. The project targets institutional use cases requiring enhanced transparency and verification features.

crypto-web3.title: Anchorage is stepping back from Robinhood and Kraken-backed stablecoin group
crypto-web3.summary: Anchorage Digital CEO Nathan McCauley announced the firm is withdrawing from a stablecoin initiative backed by Robinhood and Kraken, adopting a position of increased neutrality on stablecoin projects. The move reflects potential competitive or strategic considerations within the stablecoin ecosystem.

health-biotech.title: Medicare's miss on Alzheimer's drug spending
health-biotech.summary: Significantly fewer Medicare beneficiaries than anticipated are adopting recently approved Alzheimer's disease medications, resulting in lower-than-expected drug spending. The lower-than-projected uptake raises questions about patient access, physician prescribing patterns, and the commercial viability of these costly new therapies.

health-biotech.title: CSL sinks to nine-year low on revenue warning, $5B write-down
health-biotech.summary: CSL Limited announced a 4% reduction in full-year revenue guidance and disclosed a $5 billion impairment charge, sending the company's stock to its lowest level since 2017. The significant financial setback reflects challenges in the company's product portfolio or recent acquisitions.

health-biotech.title: Supreme Court temporarily extends women's access to a widely used abortion pill
health-biotech.summary: The Supreme Court has temporarily maintained access to mifepristone, a widely used abortion medication, while the court deliberates on whether to permit proposed restrictions on the drug. The decision provides continued regulatory stability for the medication while legal proceedings continue.

health-biotech.title: Rezdiffra is exceeding Wall Street's expectations as the MASH market takes shape
health-biotech.summary: Rezdiffra is outperforming investor expectations in the metabolic-associated fatty liver disease market, demonstrating room for multiple competitive therapies even as Novo Nordisk's Wegovy expands into the indication. The commercial success suggests robust market demand for MASH treatments with different mechanisms of action.

health-biotech.title: European regulators greenlight Fractyl Health's clinical test of GLP-1 gene therapy
health-biotech.summary: European regulators have approved Fractyl Health to initiate clinical testing of a GLP-1 gene therapy designed to potentially replace chronic injectable and oral GLP-1 medications with a single-dose treatment. The authorization represents a significant milestone in the development of gene therapy approaches to chronic metabolic disease management.

health-biotech.title: Daiichi eyes almost $15B in oncology sales by 2030 in ADC franchise push
health-biotech.summary: Daiichi Sankyo projects its oncology franchise, anchored by antibody-drug conjugate therapies, will generate approximately $15 billion in sales by 2030 as part of its broader strategic growth plan. The ambitious revenue target reflects the company's confidence in its ADC pipeline and the expanding market for targeted cancer therapies.

health-biotech.title: Colombia wins a key court ruling over a compulsory license issued for an HIV medicine
health-biotech.summary: A South American court upheld Colombia's government decision to issue a compulsory license for an HIV medication, affirming the nation's authority to prioritize public health access over patent protections. The ruling represents a significant victory for generic drug availability in resource-limited settings.

health-biotech.title: Inhibrx says combo therapy shrank more tumors than Merck's Keytruda alone
health-biotech.summary: Biotech company Inhibrx reports that its experimental drug INBRX-106 combined with Merck's Keytruda demonstrated superior tumor shrinkage compared to Keytruda monotherapy in patients with metastatic head and neck cancer. The positive interim data supports continued development of the combination approach in this difficult-to-treat malignancy.

health-biotech.title: Optum Rx unveils pharmacy model with clear fees, building on transparency push
health-biotech.summary: UnitedHealth Group's Optum Rx introduced a new prescription drug benefit model launching in 2027 that aims to increase transparency across the pharmaceutical supply chain with explicit fee structures. The initiative reflects growing industry momentum toward clearer pricing visibility and reduced hidden costs for consumers and plan sponsors.

health-biotech.title: GSK cuts deal with Sino to broaden reach of hepatitis B drug
health-biotech.summary: GlaxoSmithKline entered into a marketing agreement with a China-based partner to expand access to bepirovirsen, its hepatitis B therapy, in key Asian markets. The partnership represents part of GSK's broader strategy to leverage regional relationships and accelerate market penetration for its viral disease portfolio.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group has launched a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes and coursework across U.S. schools and universities. The attackers defaced the service's login page with ransom demands and threatened to leak data belonging to 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
cybersecurity.summary: Google disclosed the first known malicious use of artificial intelligence to develop a zero-day exploit, with cybercrime threat actors leveraging AI to bypass two-factor authentication at scale. This marks a significant escalation in attack sophistication, demonstrating AI's capability in vulnerability discovery and exploit generation for real-world attacks.

cybersecurity.title: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 Under Active Exploitation to Deploy Filemanager Backdoor
cybersecurity.summary: A threat actor named Mr_Rot13 has been actively exploiting a critical cPanel vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) to deploy a Filemanager backdoor on compromised systems. The flaw affects cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) and allows attackers to bypass authentication and gain elevated control over affected environments.

cybersecurity.title: TeamPCP Compromises Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Weeks After KICS Supply Chain Attack
cybersecurity.summary: Checkmarx confirmed that a malicious version of its Jenkins Application Security Testing plugin was published to the Jenkins Marketplace, representing a supply chain compromise. Users are advised to update to version 2.0.13-829.vc72453fa_1c16 or earlier to avoid the compromised builds.

cybersecurity.title: Fake OpenAI Privacy Filter Repo Hits #1 on Hugging Face, Draws 244K Downloads
cybersecurity.summary: A malicious repository impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter achieved trending status on Hugging Face and accumulated 244,000 downloads by delivering a Rust-based information stealer to Windows users. The fake project, named Open-OSS/privacy-filter, copied OpenAI's legitimate release details to deceive developers into downloading the compromised version.

cybersecurity.title: TCLBANKER Banking Trojan Targets Financial Platforms via WhatsApp and Outlook Worms
cybersecurity.summary: A previously undocumented Brazilian banking trojan called TCLBANKER has been identified targeting 59 financial institutions, fintech platforms, and cryptocurrency exchanges through worm-based distribution via WhatsApp and Outlook. The malware is assessed as a major update of the Maverick trojan family and spreads through the SORVEPOTEL worm.

cybersecurity.title: ShinyHunters Claims Second Attack Against Instructure
cybersecurity.summary: The threat actor ShinyHunters has claimed responsibility for a second attack against Instructure (the parent company of Canvas), creating additional complications for the organization's breach response efforts. Hundreds of millions of individuals' personally identifiable information remains at risk from the ongoing incidents.

cybersecurity.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers identified 28 fraudulent applications on Google Play Store claiming to provide call history access but instead deceiving users into paid subscriptions providing no legitimate value. The apps accumulated over 7.3 million downloads, with one application alone accounting for millions of fraudulent charges.

cybersecurity.title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
cybersecurity.summary: A privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed 'Dirty Frag,' similar to previous Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, poses a significant threat to enterprise Linux distributions and may already be under limited exploitation. The flaw enables attackers to escalate privileges on vulnerable systems.

cybersecurity.title: GM Agrees to $12.75M California Settlement Over Sale of Drivers' Data
cybersecurity.summary: General Motors has agreed to a $12.75 million settlement with California Attorney General Rob Bonta for violating the California Consumer Privacy Act by selling driver data without proper consent. The settlement resolves allegations that GM mishandled consumer personal information in commercial transactions.

startups-vc.title: Blitzy Raises $200M At $1.4B Valuation For Autonomous Software Development
startups-vc.summary: Blitzy, a Cambridge-based autonomous software development startup, has secured $200 million in funding at a $1.4 billion valuation, bringing its total raise to over $204 million. The funding reflects continued investor confidence in AI-powered developer tools designed to streamline enterprise coding and software development.

startups-vc.title: Billion-Dollar AI Rounds Push April To Third-Highest Startup Funding Month In A Year
startups-vc.summary: Global venture funding reached $56 billion in April 2026, marking the third-largest monthly funding total in the past year, with growth driven primarily by a handful of large billion-dollar AI rounds. The month's funding was up 100 percent year-over-year, signaling accelerating investor appetite for AI startups.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Defense Tech Leads With Multiple Large Deals, Topped By $600M For Space Security Startup True Anomaly
startups-vc.summary: Defense and space security startup True Anomaly led the week's largest venture funding rounds with a $600 million raise, reflecting growing investor focus on defense tech. The week also saw substantial funding for startups applying AI to fintech, marketing, customer service, healthcare, and developer tools.

startups-vc.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
startups-vc.summary: Robinhood Markets has confidentially filed for a second venture fund that will target both growth-stage and early-stage startups amid the continued AI investment boom. The move marks the retail trading platform's expansion into venture capital and fund management.

startups-vc.title: The "people's airline" and the enterprise AI gold rush
startups-vc.summary: Major AI companies including Anthropic and OpenAI are launching new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment, while large software firms like SAP are acquiring AI startups, with SAP's $1 billion acquisition of German startup Prior Labs exemplifying the trend. The acceleration of enterprise AI deals indicates that startups building enterprise tools have become prime acquisition targets.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Enterprise AI, Space Tech And Biotech Top The Ranks
startups-vc.summary: Enterprise AI, space technology, and biotech dominated the week's largest funding rounds, continuing the trend of major capital concentration in these sectors. The funding activity underscores sustained investor momentum across multiple high-growth technology categories.

startups-vc.title: Korea's biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
startups-vc.summary: Config, backed by Korea's largest manufacturers, is positioning itself as a critical infrastructure provider for robotics by focusing on high-quality training data rather than building robots directly. The startup's approach reflects the growing recognition that data quality is fundamental to advancing AI-driven robotics capabilities.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Sales And Marketing Gets An AI Makeover
startups-vc.summary: Sales, marketing, and CRM startups have attracted approximately $2.7 billion in seed through growth-stage funding globally so far in 2026, demonstrating strong investor appetite for AI-enhanced go-to-market solutions. The funding surge reflects accelerating adoption of AI tools across the sales and marketing technology landscape.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Learnings from Monday at $1.4 Billion in ARR: Guidance Raised, Stock Crushes, NDR Up to 116%, But Real AI Revenue Still Ahead
startups-vc.summary: Monday.com reported strong Q1 2026 results with guidance raised and a 28 percent stock surge in pre-market trading, demonstrating market enthusiasm for enterprise software with AI capabilities. Despite both Monday.com and HubSpot reporting revenue beats with similar customer bases and AI strategies, their divergent stock reactions highlight investors' nuanced evaluation of AI monetization progress.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has appointed Harshita Arora as a General Partner, bringing deep fintech and infrastructure experience along with founding experience dating back to her teenage years. The addition strengthens YC's investment expertise in critical infrastructure sectors.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 8 May 2026: EU eyes fossil-fuel exemptions | Wind and solar save UK '£1.7bn' | Amazon 'tipping point'
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major developments in climate policy and renewable energy, including EU considerations of fossil-fuel exemptions and quantified economic benefits of wind and solar deployment in the UK. The digest also addresses concerns regarding Amazon rainforest reaching potential ecological tipping points.

energy-climate.title: Q&A: How countries got the global 'net-zero' shipping deal 'back on track'
energy-climate.summary: International negotiations have resumed progress toward establishing a comprehensive framework for reducing global shipping emissions, a critical sector accounting for approximately 3% of worldwide greenhouse gas emissions. The agreement represents a significant diplomatic achievement in addressing hard-to-decarbonize industries.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 1 May 2026: Countries chart path away from fossil fuels | China's clean-tech surge | Global forest loss slows
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest reports on coordinated global efforts to transition away from fossil fuels, China's expanding dominance in clean technology manufacturing and deployment, and progress in slowing deforestation rates. These developments signal accelerating momentum across multiple dimensions of climate action.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: What the UK car industry is not saying about EV targets
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief investigates claims from the UK automotive sector regarding electric vehicle adoption constraints, revealing discrepancies between industry messaging and available demand data. The analysis provides evidence-based context for ongoing debates over EV transition timelines and feasibility.

energy-climate.title: Egypt installs 800 MW of solar in 2025
energy-climate.summary: Egypt achieved 800 MW of solar capacity installations in 2025, with projections indicating annual additions will exceed 2 GW in coming years and reach 3.5 GW-plus by 2029, growing cumulative capacity from 2.9 GW to 34.3 GW by 2035. This trajectory positions Egypt as a significant renewable energy developer in the Middle East and North Africa region.

energy-climate.title: Adani adds 150 MW solar capacity to Khavda 30 GW renewable energy park
energy-climate.summary: Adani Green Energy has commissioned a 150 MW solar installation at the Khavda renewable energy complex in Gujarat, advancing progress toward the facility's 30 GW planned capacity by 2029. The project represents significant progress in India's renewable energy deployment strategy.

energy-climate.title: BloombergNEF confirms energy storage has reached the 100 GW era
energy-climate.summary: BloombergNEF reports that global energy storage capacity has reached 100 GW, marking a milestone achievement in battery and storage technology deployment. The milestone reflects strengthening economics and policy support driving accelerated deployment across multiple markets.

energy-climate.title: Assessment of Nigeria's agrivoltaic potential identifies northern states as optimal areas
energy-climate.summary: A geospatial analysis identifies northern Nigerian states as having exceptional potential for agrivoltaic systems, with high solar resources, abundant cropland, and limited electrification that could be addressed through dual-use installations on less than 1.5% of cultivated land. The assessment indicates agrivoltaics could substantially advance energy access and agricultural productivity simultaneously.

energy-climate.title: Neoenergia plans $10bn investment in Brazil by 2030
energy-climate.summary: Neoenergia, the Brazilian subsidiary of Spanish utility Iberdrola, has renewed three electricity distribution concessions and announced approximately $10.2 billion in investments through 2030. This commitment signals sustained capital deployment toward modernizing Brazil's electricity infrastructure and supporting renewable energy integration.

energy-climate.title: Ireland urged to create national clearing house to streamline solar projects
energy-climate.summary: Solar Ireland's CEO has called for establishment of a national clearing house to accelerate ground-mounted photovoltaic project deployment, citing robust investor interest and pipeline constraints stemming from coordination challenges in early project development stages. Such institutional coordination could reduce development timelines and unlock deployment potential.

asia-pacific.title: 10 injured in truck-bus crash outside Hong Kong shopping centre
asia-pacific.summary:
Ten people have been injured in a crash involving a truck and a bus outside a shopping centre in Hong Kong’s Kwun Tong.
Police said they received a report of the crash in Sau Mau Ping at about 6.20am on Tuesday, where a truck collided with a KMB double-decker bus, leaving eight passengers and the two drivers with mild injuries.
The collision occurred outside Po Tat Shopping Centre on Po Lam Road, the force said.
Part of Po Lam Road towards Sau Mau Ping Road was closed but reopened at...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Are you still hiring?’: K-pop king G-Dragon gifts flats to long-serving staff, sparks online buzz
asia-pacific.summary:
K-pop superstar G-Dragon’s decision to gift his long-serving staff members flats worth a combined total of hundreds of millions of won (around several hundred thousand USD) has sparked a frenzy among netizens, with some asking, “Are you still hiring?”
Dubbed the “King of K-pop,” the 38-year-old singer Kwon Ji-yong, better known as G-Dragon in the entertainment industry, is one of the most popular stars in South Korea and across Asia. He is the leader of BigBang, one of the world’s bestselling...

asia-pacific.title: Acclaimed biologist Xu Xianzhong returns to China after arrests in his US lab
asia-pacific.summary:
Renowned biologist Xu Xianzhong has returned to China, taking up a full-time position in the southern tech hub of Shenzhen, months after four researchers in his laboratory at the University of Michigan were accused by the United States of “biological material smuggling”.
Xu, a fellow of the prestigious American Association for the Advancement of Science – has joined the Shenzhen Medical Academy of Research and Translation (SMART) as a senior investigator with its Institute of Bio-Architecture...

asia-pacific.title: How many ride-hailing permits should Hong Kong allow? That depends on who you ask
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong taxi trade leaders and Uber have both expressed concerns over a looming quota for ride-hailing vehicles, despite transport authorities vowing to consult stakeholders before setting the final figure.
They were responding to the Transport and Logistics Bureau’s submission to lawmakers of regulatory details for the industry on Monday.
It pledged to include the quota in the final gazetted document, pending lawmakers’ approval before their summer recess in mid-July.
Platforms Uber, Tada,...

asia-pacific.title: Japan’s new spy agency receives FBI backing with eyes on China and Russia
asia-pacific.summary:
Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is pushing through legislation to establish the country’s first centralised intelligence agency since the second world war, driven by concerns that its existing set-up is too fragmented to keep pace with espionage, cyberthreats and “grey zone” operations.
The plan, recently endorsed by the US Federal Bureau of Investigation, would transform the Cabinet Intelligence and Research Office (CIRO) into a central hub, drawing in analysts, technologists and...

asia-pacific.title: Southern California mayor will plead guilty to acting as agent for Chinese government
asia-pacific.summary:
A Southern California mayor has agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government, and has resigned from her city position, officials said on Monday.
Eileen Wang, the mayor of Arcadia, was charged in April with one count of acting in the United States as an illegal agent of a foreign government. She was accused of doing the bidding of Chinese officials, such as sharing articles favourable to Beijing, without prior notification to the US government as required by...

asia-pacific.title: Netflix sued by Texas for allegedly spying on children, addicting users
asia-pacific.summary:
Netflix was sued on Monday by Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who accused the streaming company of spying on children and other consumers by collecting their data ‌without consent and designing its platform to be addictive.
The lawsuit says that for years, Netflix has falsely represented to consumers that it did not collect or share user data, when it actually tracked and sold viewers’ habits and preferences to commercial data brokers and advertising technology companies, making billions of..

asia-pacific.title: US trial hears witness claims of harassment over Chinese ‘secret police station’
asia-pacific.summary:
A US government witness in a trial of an alleged illegal Chinese police station in New York testified on Monday that he was harassed after holding a protest against establishing the Fuzhou-directed facility, one of several that China purportedly set up overseas.
The US government has charged Lu Jianwang, 64, with acting as an unauthorised agent of China; conspiracy to act as a foreign agent and obstruction of justice. Prosecutors argue that it is part of a pattern that underscores China’s bid to

asia-pacific.title: ‘Art of the deal’: UK telecoms exit fits Li family’s trademark timing for selling at top
asia-pacific.summary:
CK Hutchison Holdings’ shares climbed to their highest level since 2020 after the conglomerate announced plans to exit the UK mobile market, signalling that investors believe the Li family may once again have timed an industry peak before the broader market.
Shares rose about 12 per cent to HK$73.30 on Monday from the May 5 close, after the company said it would sell its 49 per cent stake in VodafoneThree for US$5.8 billion. CK Hutchison said the disposal was expected to generate a gain of about

asia-pacific.title: China’s AI ascent leaves Trump a stark choice: escalate or relax chip controls?
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s landmark visit to China comes as the US-Iran war disrupts global energy supplies, fuels economic uncertainty and adds fresh strain to Washington-Beijing ties. In this story, part of a series examining how rivalry, interdependence and geopolitical crises are reshaping the relationship between the two powers, we examine how artificial intelligence (AI), chip controls and competing technology ecosystems are redefining US-China rivalry.
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was the...
